task_completion.description: Verify that the title 'Database Technology Evaluation — Q4 2024' is added to the document.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the text 'Database Technology Evaluation — Q4 2024' placed prominently on the EdrawMax canvas.

task_completion.description: Verify that a 4x7 grid/table is created with the correct headers.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a table structure with 4 columns and 7 rows. The top row must contain the headers 'Criteria', 'PostgreSQL', 'MySQL', and 'MongoDB'.

task_completion.description: Verify that the matrix rows are populated with the correct criteria and database evaluation data.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for row labels like 'License Type', 'ACID Compliance', 'Horizontal Scalability', 'JSON Support', 'Community Size', and 'Overall Recommendation', along with their respective data (e.g., 'BSD (Free)', 'Full', 'Native', 'SSPL') in the correct columns.

task_completion.description: Verify that the header row is formatted with a dark background and bold white text.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the top row of the table; the cell background should be a dark color (e.g., dark blue, black, dark grey) and the text inside should be white and bold.

task_completion.description: Verify that the data cells are color-coded based on the strength of the attributes.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for green backgrounds on positive/strong traits (e.g., 'Full', 'Native'), red backgrounds on negative/weak traits (e.g., 'Limited', 'Partial'), and yellow backgrounds on moderate traits (e.g., 'Basic').

task_completion.description: Verify that the diagram is saved as an EDDX file and exported as a PDF.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for save/export dialog boxes, EdrawMax tab names, or a file manager/terminal window showing 'db_evaluation_matrix.eddx' and 'db_evaluation_matrix.pdf' in the /home/ga/Documents/ directory.

integrity.description: Verify that the EdrawMax graphical user interface was used to create the matrix.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for screenshots showing the EdrawMax application open with the matrix being actively edited on the canvas, rather than terminal commands generating the files.

integrity.description: Verify that the matrix is built using EdrawMax table or shape tools.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for table selection handles, cell editing cursors, or formatting panels being used, ensuring the agent didn't just paste a pre-made image of a table.

integrity.description: Verify that the PDF export is performed through the EdrawMax interface.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for the EdrawMax 'Export & Send' or 'Save As' dialogs being used to create the PDF, rather than command-line conversion tools.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Verified target output paths are '/home/ga/Documents/db_evaluation_matrix.eddx' (minimum 5000 bytes) and '/home/ga/Documents/db_evaluation_matrix.pdf' (minimum 10000 bytes). The table must contain specific text including 'PostgreSQL', 'MySQL', 'MongoDB', 'ACID', 'Scalability', 'JSON', 'BSD', 'SSPL', and 'Native'.
